scope: 0705 + 0706 post-recovery-3 verification (vskill-platform src/, vskill src/, vskill CI workflow)
mode: direct-orchestrator (3-reviewer default, Task subagent spawning unavailable in this environment)
context: Verification review of closed increments 0705-vskill-versioning-multi-file-diff + 0706-vskill-cli-windows-compat. Both status=completed. Scope limited to files touched by hardening/recovery commits (F-001..F-009, F-CR-001..007, F-CR-2A..2D).

verifiedFixes.fix: VSKILL_API_BASE threaded via resolveBaseUrl() in reportInstall, checkUpdates, reportInstallBatch, compareVersions
verifiedFixes.testCoverage: client.test.ts L452, L565, L631, L740 (F-CR-001 + per-call env override tests)

verifiedFixes.fix: versionCreated flag gates post-write steps (submission link, search-index invalidate, queue dispatch) so they never run when SkillVersion create/update failed
verifiedFixes.testCoverage: covered indirectly via publish-degraded-data.test.ts which asserts no post-write KV puts fire after DegradedDataError

verifiedFixes.fix: buildLocalContentFile returns status=unavailable when both sides empty (instead of misleading 'removed')
filterBySkillPath enforces root-only filtering for repo-root SKILL.md; previously '/SKILL.md' normalized to '' and leaked every file
verifiedFixes.testCoverage: compare.test.ts L83, L95 (F-CR-004 root-only filter tests)

verifiedFixes.fix: skillPath traversal guard rejects backslash separators, URL-encoded traversal (%2e/%2f), CRLF injection before fetch
verifiedFixes.testCoverage: backfill test L389-L453 (F-CR-005 covers 6 attack shapes)

verifiedFixes.fix: compareVersions encodes hierarchical skill segments individually (split / encode / rejoin) so '/' separators survive while spaces/# get encoded
P2025 race in P2002 catch path now retries upsert once, then falls back to {kind:pending, id:''} instead of surfacing a Prisma 500
backfill treeHash uses canonical computeTreeHash({'SKILL.md': content}) instead of sha256(JSON.stringify(manifest)) — ensures parity with submit path, prevents spurious re-enqueues
manifest[].size uses Buffer.byteLength(content,'utf8') instead of content.length — fixes UTF-16 vs UTF-8 divergence on emoji/CJK
publishSkill refuses to persist SkillVersion with empty contentHash — throws DegradedDataError that rolls back the transaction and logs for investigation
verifiedFixes.testCoverage: publish-degraded-data.test.ts L135,191,240 (3 test cases covering both-missing, GitHub-fallback, and partial-missing paths)

verifiedFixes.fix: githubCompare validates response shape (Array.isArray(data.files)) for BOTH cache-hit and upstream paths before returning; corrupt/drift responses return null instead of propagating undefined files
fetchSkillContent uses skillPath so subfolder skills hash the correct file (not repo-root SKILL.md)
republish detection compares EITHER contentHash OR treeHash — script-only changes (scripts/install.sh) now correctly re-enqueue even when SKILL.md byte-identical
queue.send runs FIRST, state update SECOND; queue.send failure keeps row PUBLISHED and returns null so caller falls back to 'verified'
headSha uses toRow.gitSha (caller-requested head) instead of compare.merge_base_commit.sha (common ancestor) — fixes cache-key corruption for diverged branches
ghost-guard in-place update path now writes content + contentHash + treeHash + manifest (not just cert metadata) when contentHash differs but gitSha placeholder — preserves authors' edits
from/to query params validated with semver-or-SHA regex BEFORE DB lookup — rejects malformed strings with 400
verifiedFixes.testCoverage: route.test.ts L270,L285 (0705 F-008 pre-DB validation + semver-prerelease acceptance)

verifiedFixes.fix: fetchAtSha rejects path-traversal + leading-slash (later widened by F-CR-005 to cover backslash/URL-encoded/CRLF)
verifiedFixes.testCoverage: backfill test L337 (0705 F-009 + F-CR-005 combined suite)

verifiedFixes.fix: install-smoke uses `-y` flag (correct), no `|| true` masking, exit-code check PLUS output-shape grep so silent failures exit 0 still fail CI
verifiedFixes.testCoverage: CI itself is the test — the grep assertion against known branches is the regression guard

knownDebt.description: Asymmetric baseSha/headSha sourcing — baseSha reads from GitHub compare echo (compare.base_commit.sha ?? fromRow.gitSha), headSha reads from DB (toRow.gitSha). Values can diverge under force-push. Display-only fields; no functional impact on dedup/diff logic.
knownDebt.rubricAcceptance: .specweave/increments/0705-vskill-versioning-multi-file-diff/rubric.md — recovery-3 accepted as debt rather than expand scope

observations: Every F-CR finding from the prior round has at least one explicit regression test anchored by the F-CR ID in a test description or comment — high protection against silent regressions.

DegradedDataError (F-CR-2C) is intentionally swallowed in publishSkill's outer catch (publish.ts:548-554) rather than re-thrown. The behavior is deliberate: the row is not persisted, post-write steps are gated by versionCreated, and the logged error surfaces for ops investigation. This choice is documented in errors.ts:49-58 and is correct given the outer catch only re-raises MonotonicityError (which needs HTTP 409 surfacing).

The F-CR-005 path-traversal regex (`(^|[/\\])\.\.([/\\]|$)|^[/\\]|%2e|%2f|[\r\n]`) does not reject Windows drive-letter prefixes (e.g., 'C:\foo'), but this is acceptable — raw.githubusercontent.com URLs never contain drive letters, and the fetch target domain is hardcoded so any such payload would simply 404 rather than escape.

F-CR-001 env-base threading is tested four separate times (reportInstall, checkUpdates, reportInstallBatch, compareVersions) — each call site individually verifies VSKILL_API_BASE override. No call site bypasses resolveBaseUrl().

compare.ts (github) does not URL-encode owner/repo/base/head path segments (line 167). Owners/repos come from parseGitHubUrl (platform-side DB), and SHAs come from isValidSha regex — so in practice the inputs are always URL-safe. Not a finding, just noted for completeness.
Test files reference each F-CR ID in describe/it blocks, making regression discovery trivial if a future change breaks them. This discipline is worth preserving as a team norm.
conclusion: CLEAN. All 16 hardening/recovery findings (F-001..F-009, F-CR-001..007, F-CR-2A..2D) are correctly applied in the touched files and backed by explicit regression tests. F-CR-2E is documented known debt with rubric acceptance. No new critical/high/medium findings in the 0705/0706 scope. 0705 and 0706 post-recovery-3 state verified clean.
